seoTitle: Print QuickBooks Transaction List by Vendor
metaDescription: Learn how to generate and print a vendor-specific transaction list in QuickBooks to review spending and support better financial decisions.
intro: Reviewing transactions by vendor gives you a clear picture of where your money is going, which supports better financial decision-making. This guide covers generating and printing a transaction list filtered by vendor in QuickBooks.

steps.heading: Navigate to the vendor section
steps.body: Open the vendor area of QuickBooks where you can view transactions tied to specific suppliers or contractors.

steps.heading: Configure your transaction list settings
steps.body: Adjust the date range and other settings so the report captures exactly the vendor activity you want to review.

steps.heading: Preview your transaction list
steps.body: Check the generated list for accuracy before finalizing it for print or export.

steps.heading: Finalize the print process
steps.body: Print the list directly or save it as a PDF for record-keeping or sharing with your team.

faq.question: Can I filter the list by a specific date range?
faq.answer: Yes, you can adjust the report's date range to focus on any period relevant to your review, such as a quarter or fiscal year.

faq.question: Why would I need a vendor-specific transaction list?
faq.answer: It helps you track spending patterns with individual vendors, useful for budgeting, negotiating terms, or reconciling accounts payable.

faq.question: Can this report be automated?
faq.answer: Integrations like Zapier can help automate recurring report generation and reduce manual work over time.

faq.question: Is this report available for all vendor types?
faq.answer: Yes, the transaction list report generally works for any vendor recorded in your QuickBooks account, regardless of vendor type.
